narrative.headline: Fuel system complaints lead the 2022 Ford Escape
narrative.summary: Of 216 owner complaints filed with NHTSA, the fuel system is the most reported area at 59 reports (27%), tied with gasoline-related reports at 59 (27%). Service brakes follow at 33 reports (15%), then the engine at 28 (13%) and the power train at 26 (12%). Most reports describe no crash or fire, but the engine shows 4 fires and service brakes show 1 crash. Buyers should focus their inspection on the fuel system, brakes, and engine.

issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 59 reports (27% of all complaints), with no crashes or fires. Have the fuel system inspected and confirm any open recall work is complete before buying.

issues.sample: The contact owns a 2022 Ford Escape. The contact received notification of NHTSA Campaign Number: 22V859000 (Fuel System, Gasoline) however, the part to do the recall repair was unavailable. The contact stated that the manufacturer had exceeded a reasonable amount of time for the
issues.title: Gasoline
issues.blurb: Tied for the most reported area: 59 reports (27%), with no crashes or fires. These overlap with the fuel system complaints above and point to the same area to check.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The third most reported area: 33 reports (15%), including 1 crash. Test the brakes carefully and check pad and rotor condition.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 28 reports (13%), and the most safety-flagged area with 4 fires among them. Have the engine inspected thoroughly before buying.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 26 reports (12%), with no crashes or fires. Pay attention to how the transmission and drivetrain behave on a test drive.
